details:
The npm package `datefmt-helper` is a supply-chain dropper disguised as a benign date-formatting utility (its `description` reads "dates formatting utility with locale support"). The package ships no source 
  script uses HTTP / `curl` / `wget` primitives to download a second-stage payload from the hardcoded external IP `115.190.124.243`, then executes it via a Node child process — the classic download-and-execute pattern. Any 
  developer workstation or CI runner that installs the package (directly or transitively) hands arbitrary code execution to the operator of that endpoint.

Detected and classified independently by codelake Research from the 
  live npm feed on 2026-07-02; at the time of reporting the package was not present in OSV or GHSA (a first-catch).
